dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7406 IH: Meeting Demand for Organic Produce Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Newhouse introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Organic Foods Production Act of 1990 to provide producers with the option to confirm the absence of prohibited substances through testing, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Meeting Demand for Organic Produce Act.

section.header: Option to confirm the absence of prohibited substances through testing

subsection.text: Section 2105 of the Organic Foods Production Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 6504) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking To be and inserting the following:
in paragraph (1), by inserting shall before have been;

clause.text: by striking products; and and inserting product; or;
by striking livestock, not and inserting the following:
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: shall be produced in soil or another growing medium that has been confirmed prior to the time of planting to contain no prohibited substances, including synthetic chemicals, through testing or other methods certified by the Secretary to verify the absence of prohibited substances in the growing medium; and
in paragraph (3), by inserting shall before be produced; and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of the Meeting Demand for Organic Produce Act, the Secretary shall issue an advanced notice of proposed rulemaking to develop guidelines and criteria for approved verification methods described in subsection (a)(2)(B) to ensure accuracy and reliability with respect to the testing described in that subsection.
Section 2114(f) of the Organic Foods Production Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 6513(f)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (4) as subparagraphs (A) through (D), respectively, and indenting appropriately;
in the matter preceding subparagraph (A) (as so redesignated), by striking An organic and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: by adding or after the semicolon at the end;
by striking include a 3 year and inserting the following:
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: a certification that the soil or other growing medium of the area has been confirmed prior to the time of planting to contain no prohibited substances, including synthetic chemicals, through testing or other methods certified by the Secretary to verify the absence of prohibited substances in the growing medium;

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

Not later than 60 days after the date of enactment of the Meeting Demand for Organic Produce Act, the Secretary shall issue an advanced notice of proposed rulemaking to develop guidelines and criteria for approved verification methods described in paragraph (1)(B)(ii) to ensure accuracy and reliability with respect to the testing described in that paragraph.
